title: Matt Sing

Playing Career - Summary|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=25 May 2023}}{{Cite web|url=http://ww16.yesterdayshero.com.au/PlayerProfile_Matt-Sing_154.aspx?sub1=20230525-2357-1747-b7ae-e2d4ff89db78|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120816232938/http://www.yesterdayshero.com.au/PlayerProfile_Matt-Sing_154.aspx|url-status=dead|title=Yesterday's Hero - Your Sporting Hero's - Matt Sing - North Queensland, Penrith, Sydney Roosters|archive-date=16 August 2012|website=ww16.yesterdayshero.com.au|access-date=25 May 2023}} }} '''Matthew Charles Sing''' (born 13 March 1975) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. An [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian]] and [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] representative, Sing played his club football for the [[Penrith Panthers]], [[Sydney Roosters]] and [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL) and for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]]. Sing was the sixth player in Australian first grade history to score 150 tries and, as of 2025, is equal tenth on the [[List of players with 100 NRL tries|all-time try scorers list]].{{Cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/top-10-indigenous-nrl-tryscorers-of-all-time/ae796ee5-f1d9-4777-a5d7-1535bfdd388c|title = Top 10 Indigenous NRL Try Scorers - Nine Wide World of Sports| date=10 May 2017 }} ==Background== Born in [[Winton, Queensland]], Sing is of [[Indigenous Australian]] descent.{{cite web |url=https://www.deadlyvibe.com.au/2007/11/matt-sing/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200918083152/https://www.deadlyvibe.com.au/2007/11/matt-sing/ |archive-date=18 September 2020 |title=Matt Sing - Deadly Vibe}} He grew up in [[Dysart, Queensland|Dysart]], playing his junior rugby league for the Dysart Bulls and later attending [[North Rockhampton State High School]]{{Cite news|url=https://www.themorningbulletin.com.au/news/nrlstarspasson-advicetostudents/1537686/|title = NRL stars return to school|newspaper = The Courier Mail|date = 9 September 2012}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.themorningbulletin.com.au/news/apn-sing-lauds-origin/67958/|title = Sing lauds Origin tour|newspaper = The Courier Mail|date = 18 May 2005}} In 1992, Sing was selected for the Queensland under-17 side, coming off the bench in their 14–22 loss to New South Wales.{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/representative/under-17s-origin/1992-2/|title = 1992|date = 25 October 2017}} ==Playing career== ===Penrith Panthers=== Sing joined the [[Penrith Panthers]] in 1993, originally as a {{rlp|hb}}. After poor form for the Panthers' lower grade sides, he was dropped to the local [[Penrith District Rugby League|Penrith]] competition, where former Panthers' coach [[Len Stacker]] moved him to {{rlp|ce}}.{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/matt-sing/summary.html|title = Matt Sing - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project}} The move kick-started his career, as he returned to the Panthers' reserve grade side after just one game, was selected for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] and made his first grade debut in Round 13 of the [[1993 NSWRL season]]. He finished his debut season playing four games off the bench. In 1994, he locked down a starting centre spot for Penrith, playing 17 games, scoring six tries and being named the club's Rookie of the Year.{{Cite web|url=https://themightypanthers.wordpress.com/2019/04/21/players-matt-sing/|title=PLAYERS | Matt Sing|date=20 April 2019}} In 1995, Sing made his [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] debut for [[Queensland Maroons|Queensland]], starting on the wing in all three games in their 3–0 series victory over [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]]. In July 1995, he made his Test debut for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], starting on the wing in their 20–10 win over [[New Zealand Kiwis|New Zealand]]. Sing left the Panthers at the end of the 1995 season as the club sided with the rebel [[Super League (Australia)|Super League]] competition. Sing stayed loyal to the [[Australian Rugby League]], signing with the ARL-aligned [[Sydney City Roosters]]. In Sing's final season for the Panthers, he scored 8 tries in 15 games. ===Sydney Roosters=== In [[1996 ARL season|1996]], in his first season for the Roosters, Sing scored 12 tries in 21 games. In his first five seasons with the club, Sing scored 10 or more tries each year. He played in all three games for Queensland in their [[1996 State of Origin series]] loss to New South Wales and in October, represented Australia in their 52–6 win over [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]]. In [[1997 ARL season|1997]], he scored 11 tries in 22 games as the Roosters came within one game of the [[1997 ARL Grand Final|Grand Final]]. In June 1997, he scored his first try for Queensland in their 14–15 loss to New South Wales. In July 1997, he scored for Australia in their 28–8 win over the Rest of the World. In [[1998 NRL season|1998]], Sing scored 15 tries in 27 games for the Roosters and once again played all three games for Queensland in their series win over New South Wales. In Round 21 of the 1998 season, he played his 100th first grade game in the Roosters' 20–32 loss to the [[Melbourne Storm]]. In 1999, he earned a recall to the Australian side, coming off the bench in their [[1999 Anzac Test|Anzac Test]] win over New Zealand. In 2000, he was awarded the [[Australian Sports Medal]] for his contribution to Australia's international standing in the sport of rugby league. On 27 August 2000, he started on the wing for the Roosters in their [[2000 NRL Grand Final|Grand Final]] loss to the [[Brisbane Broncos]]. In Round 5 of the [[2001 NRL season]], he played his 150th first grade in the Roosters' 30–31 loss to the [[Northern Eagles]]. ===North Queensland Cowboys=== In [[2002 NRL season|2002]], Sing joined the struggling [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. In his first season with the club, he scored 16 tries in 23 games and won their Player of the Year and Players' Player awards. In Round 22 of the 2002 season, he scored his 100th first grade try in the Cowboys' 30–40 loss to the [[Melbourne Storm]]. In [[2003 NRL season]], he scored 21 tries in 20 games and again won the club's Players' Player award. That season he earned recalls to the Queensland and Australian sides. In the third game of the [[2003 State of Origin series]], he scored a hat-trick in a 36–6 win over New South Wales and was named man of the match.https://www.qrl.com.au/maroons-origin-history/ {{Dead link|date=February 2022}} In November 2003, he started two games on the wing in Australia's [[2003 Kangaroo tour of Great Britain and France|Ashes series]] win over [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]]. In [[2004 NRL season|2004]], Sing played just 12 games due to jaw and knee injuries.{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/lonesome-cowboy-sing-has-no-regrets-20041212-gdkatd.html|title = Lonesome Cowboy Sing has no regrets|date = 12 December 2004}} He returned in time for the finals, scoring a hat trick in a 30–22 win over the [[Canterbury Bulldogs]], the club's first ever finals game.{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/the-seven-biggest-finals-upsets-of-the-nrl-era/news-story/0a65ac80dfc61ccc1db418fe98ea53e9|title = The seven biggest NRL finals upsets|newspaper = Daily Telegraph|date = 15 September 2015}} In October and November 2004, he represented Australia in their victorious [[2004 Rugby League Tri-Nations|Tri-Nations]] campaign, starting on the wing in all five games. Following the Tri-Nations, he announced his retirement from representative football.{{Cite web|url=https://leagueunlimited.com/news/matt-sing-hangs-up-rep-boots/|title=Matt Sing hangs up rep boots » League Unlimited|website=League Unlimited|accessdate=25 May 2023}} In 2005, Sing reversed his retirement decision, starting all three games for Queensland in the [[2005 State of Origin series]] and playing for Australia in the [[2005 Anzac Test]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/rauhihi-urges-sing-to-play-rep-footy-20050411-gdl3oz.html|title = Rauhihi urges Sing to play rep footy|date = 10 April 2005}} In the [[2005 NRL season]], he played 25 games, scoring 16 tries. He played his 250th first grade game in the Cowboys' 29–0 preliminary final win over the [[Parramatta Eels]]. On 2 October 2005, he started on the wing in the Cowboys' [[2005 NRL Grand Final|Grand Final]] loss to the [[Wests Tigers]]. In 2006, Sing played his final season for the Cowboys, playing 24 games and finishing as the side's top try scorer with 13 tries. In Round 2 of the [[2006 NRL season]], he scored his 150th first grade try, the sixth player to do so. On 6 June 2006, he signed a two-year deal with [[Hull F.C.]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/sport/content/200606/s1657989.htm|title = ABC Sport - Rugby League - Sing signs for Hull|website = [[Australian Broadcasting Corporation]]}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/on-song-hull-tie-up-deal-for-cowboy-sing-20060606-gdnp0r.html|title=On-song Hull tie up deal for Cowboy Sing|date=6 June 2006}} ===Hull F.C.=== In 2007, Sing scored 11 tries in 23 [[Super League]] games for [[Hull F.C.]] On 30 August 2008, he started on the wing in Hull's [[2008 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/challenge-cup-2008|title = Challenge Cup 2008 | Sport | the Guardian|website = [[TheGuardian.com]]}} He retired from professional rugby league at the end of the 2008 season.{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/hull/7442784.stm|title = No plans to stay in rugby - Sing|date = 8 June 2008}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2008/sep/07/superleague.rugbyleague|title=Sing and Hill begin journeys into the sunset|website=[[TheGuardian.com]]|date=6 September 2008}} He played 48 games for Hull over two seasons, scoring 17 tries. ==Achievements and accolades== ===Individual=== '''[[Penrith Panthers Honours|Penrith Panthers Rookie of the Year]]:''' 1994
'''[[List of North Queensland Cowboys honours#Club awards|North Queensland Cowboys Player of the Year]]:''' 2002

colspan=2| Career totals ! 24 ! 5 ! 0 ! — ! 0 ! 20 |} ==Post-playing career== In 2009, Sing joined the [[Central Comets]] [[Queensland Cup]] side as an assistant and backs coach.{{Cite web|url=http://www.themorningbulletin.com.au/story/2009/01/28/no-swan-song/|title = Rockhampton News | the Morning Bulletin | for all your news and events around Rockhampton, Morning Bulletin has you covered. Get the latest updates on sport and local news. | the Courier Mail}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.themorningbulletin.com.au/story/2009/01/27/comets-in-full-sing/|title = Rockhampton News | the Morning Bulletin | for all your news and events around Rockhampton, Morning Bulletin has you covered. Get the latest updates on sport and local news. | the Courier Mail}} In June 2009, he briefly came out of retirement to play for the Comets.{{cite web |url=http://www.qrl.com.au/display.php?pg_id=4773 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090914012501/http://www.qrl.com.au/display.php?pg_id=4773 |archive-date=14 September 2009 |title=Queensland Rugby League:: QRL.com.au}} On 17 May 2015, Sing was named on the wing in the Cowboys 20-year team and, alongside [[Paul Bowman (rugby league)|Paul Bowman]], was an inaugural inductee into the Cowboys' Hall of Fame.{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2015/05/17/our-first-hall-of-fame-inductees/|title = Our first Hall of Fame inductees!|date = 17 May 2015}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2015/05/17/introducing-your-20-year-team/|title = Introducing your 20-year team!|date = 17 May 2015}} == References == {{reflist|40em}} == External links == *[https://www.fogs.com.au/state-of-origin/queensland-state-of-origin-players/matt-sing/ Matt Sing FOGS profile] {{2003 Kangaroo Tour squad}} {{Australia 2004 Rugby League Tri Nations squad}} {{Rugby league footballers with 150 NRL tries}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sing, Matt}} [[Category:1975 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Australia national rugby league team players]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Central Queensland Capras players]] [[Category:Hull F.C. players]] [[Category:Indigenous Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:North Queensland Cowboys players]] [[Category:Penrith Panthers players]] [[Category:People from Central Queensland]] [[Category:Queensland Rugby League State of Origin players]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]] [[Category:Recipients of the Australian Sports Medal]] [[Category:Rugby league fullbacks]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Queensland]] [[Category:Sydney Roosters players]]
